description:
Add the manual server action "BICC v5: Send SMS Queue Digest" plus a menu
entry under Recruitment → Reporting that triggers it. When fired, it emails
the HR Recruitment Manager group ONE digest of every applicant who needs an
SMS follow-up right now, grouped by job position. Each job is rendered as
its own 3-column table (Applicant name with link, mobile number, ready-to-
send SMS text with first name + company short name + job role pre-filled).

Queue gate is purely stage age + SMS-not-yet-sent. The digest does NOT
require an email reminder to have fired first — overdue applicants
appear regardless of whether the email cascade has played out, so HR
can SMS directly even when the email reminder cron is dormant on this
environment.

Three queues feeding the digest:
- Qualification (Stage 2) overdue: date_last_stage_update older than
  OVERDUE_DAYS (default 3), x_studio_sms_reminder_date null.
- Resume missing (Stage 1) overdue: date_last_stage_update older than
  OVERDUE_DAYS, attachment_number = 0, x_studio_sms_new_reminder_date null.
- Assessment (Stage 7) overdue: date_last_stage_update older than
  OVERDUE_DAYS, x_studio_assessment_sms_reminder_date null.

Also creates the three hr.applicant datetime fields the SMS dedup logic
reads — needed because they are missing on the dev database. Already
present on production via Studio so the ops no-op on the prod env.

Entry points: HR fires the digest from Recruitment → Reporting → SMS
Follow-up Queue, or from any applicant's gear-icon Action menu.

Diagnostics: the action always creates a visible mail.mail (auto_delete=
False) with a Debug block in the body listing field-existence flags,
per-queue counts, and recipient resolution.
